title: Choose Instructions, Skills, Plugins, Hooks, or MCP · Integration Triage
summary: Route recurring agent needs to the smallest durable extension surface. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in an extension-surface decision matrix and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate one-turn prompts, repository instructions, reusable skills, enforcement hooks, plugins, and external tools by scope.
Use AGENTS.md or equivalent repository guidance for durable local conventions.
Use a skill for a reusable workflow, expertise, scripts, references, or assets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""

# Add typed, bounded protocol increments below.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
Use a changed debug case derived from this module: agent ext surface choice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Scope Layers trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate one-turn prompts, repository instructions, reusable skills, enforcement hooks, plugins, and external tools by scope.

content.text: For Scope Layers, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Scope Layers precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed debug case derived from this module: agent ext surface choice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: All recurring agent behavior belongs in a skill.

stimulus.title: Scope Layers case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed debug case derived from this module: agent ext surface choice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: All recurring agent behavior belongs in a skill.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate one-turn prompts, repository instructions, reusable skills, enforcement hooks, plugins, and external tools by scope. Artifact under review: an extension-surface decision matrix.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Scope Layers, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a agent-ext-plugin-fit-yfs1vt: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Plugin Fit into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
agent-ext-plugin-fit-yfs1vt:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Plugin Fit applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use a plugin to distribute skills, connectors, hooks, and presentation assets as one installable unit.
